patch.before: Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) is now a core discipline for marketing leaders seeking relevance in AI-dominated search. Tracking brand citations, enriching schema, and leading with authoritative facts are practical levers for B2B SaaS brands to secure visibility in generative engine outputs. The brands winning AEO aren't better writers—they're better at being the cheapest-to-cite source in the room.[geo-arxiv][semrush-aeo-2024][schema-org-defined-term]
patch.after: Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) is now a core discipline for marketing leaders seeking relevance in AI-dominated search. Tracking brand citations, enriching schema, and leading with authoritative facts are practical levers for B2B SaaS brands to secure visibility in generative engine outputs. The brands winning AEO aren't better writers, they're better at being the cheapest-to-cite source in the room.[geo-arxiv][semrush-aeo-2024][schema-org-defined-term]
patch.rationale: Remove em-dashes from paragraph (reduces AI-signature density; commas preserve meaning)

instructions.evidence: 14 tricolons (4.84/500 words; target ≤2) — targeted paragraph 1/5

patch.before: Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) is the discipline of making your content eligible for direct citation, mention, or use in answers returned by AI engines like ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity. AEO differs fundamentally from traditional SEO: instead of ranking on web results, it focuses on being surfaced within the answers generated by large language models (LLMs). Optimizing for AEO requires technical, authority, and content strategies that align with how LLMs select and cite sources.[geo-arxiv][openai-cite][semrush-aeo-2024]
patch.rationale: Tricolon cadence ("X, Y, and Z") in sentence: "Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) is the discipline of making your content eligible for direct citati..." Rewrite to vary rhythm — keep meaning, avoid another three-item list.

instructions.evidence: 14 tricolons (4.84/500 words; target ≤2) — targeted paragraph 2/5

patch.before: Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) is the practice of tailoring digital content to maximize brand mentions, citations, and direct inclusion within responses generated by AI-powered answer engines. Unlike SEO, which targets search engine results pages (SERPs), AEO targets AI-generated narratives and summaries.[geo-arxiv][semrush-aeo-2024]
patch.rationale: Tricolon cadence ("X, Y, and Z") in sentence: "Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) is the practice of tailoring digital content to maximize brand ment..." Rewrite to vary rhythm — keep meaning, avoid another three-item list.

instructions.evidence: 14 tricolons (4.84/500 words; target ≤2) — targeted paragraph 3/5

patch.before: According to academic and industry analysis, citation, quotation, and statistics are the highest-impact features influencing generative engine inclusion. Structured data, especially schema.org ‘DefinedTerm’ markup, improves a brand’s likelihood of being referenced by both Google and LLMs. Demonstrating expertise, experience, authoritativeness, and trustworthiness (E-E-A-T) remains essential.[geo-arxiv][schema-org-defined-term][semrush-aeo-2024][google-search-central]
patch.rationale: Tricolon cadence ("X, Y, and Z") in sentence: "According to academic and industry analysis, citation, quotation, and statistics are the highest-imp..." Rewrite to vary rhythm — keep meaning, avoid another three-item list.

instructions.evidence: 14 tricolons (4.84/500 words; target ≤2) — targeted paragraph 4/5

patch.before: AEO is the process of optimizing your brand's content, authority signals, and structured data to increase the likelihood of being cited, quoted, or used as a trusted reference in AI-generated answers from platforms like ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.[geo-arxiv][semrush-aeo-2024]
patch.rationale: Tricolon cadence ("X, Y, and Z") in sentence: "AEO is the process of optimizing your brand's content, authority signals, and structured data to inc..." Rewrite to vary rhythm — keep meaning, avoid another three-item list.

instructions.evidence: 14 tricolons (4.84/500 words; target ≤2) — targeted paragraph 5/5

patch.before: Brands can use tools and audits to track how often their brand appears in LLM-generated answers, how frequently it gets cited, and what sentiment surrounds those mentions across platforms like ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.[semrush-aeo-2024]
patch.rationale: Tricolon cadence ("X, Y, and Z") in sentence: "Brands can use tools and audits to track how often their brand appears in LLM-generated answers, how..." Rewrite to vary rhythm — keep meaning, avoid another three-item list.

patch.target: A successful AEO strategy in 2026 includes monitoring brand citations in LLM answers, injecting schema.org ‘DefinedTerm’ and fact structures, publishing original statistics, and earning authoritative coverage.
patch.before: A successful AEO strategy in 2026 includes monitoring brand citations in LLM answers, injecting schema.org ‘DefinedTerm’ and fact structures, publishing original statistics, and earning authoritative coverage.
patch.after: Winning AEO in 2026 means three boring habits: watch your citations weekly, mark up definitions, and publish stats worth stealing.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 5/10: Magpie metaphor and 'cheapest-to-cite source' are fresh, but most sentences read like template filler.

instructions.evidence: specificity 6/10 — The post has one strong specific data point (the Semrush 34% Perplexity citation lift over 90 days) but much of the tactical advice floats in abstract verbs: 'audit,' 'track,' 'optimize,' 'monitor.' The AEO playbook section lists steps without naming a single tool, threshold, cadence, or example brand. The key-takeaways and summary repeat the same abstractions. Concrete nouns—tool names, query examples, specific schema snippet, a named brand outcome—are almost entirely absent outside the one case-study stat.

patch.target: Brands must audit, track, and optimize their visibility and sentiment in content surfaced by AI answer engines.
patch.before: Brands must audit, track, and optimize their visibility and sentiment in content surfaced by AI answer engines.
patch.after: Brands should run weekly prompts in ChatGPT-4o, Perplexity Pro, and Gemini Advanced—logging whether their brand is cited, quoted by name, or absent entirely—then compare mention-share against two named competitors using a tracking spreadsheet or a tool like Profound or Otterly.ai.
patch.rationale: Quality axis "specificity" scored 6/10: The post has one strong specific data point (the Semrush 34% Perplexity citation lift over 90 days) but much of the tactical advice floats in abstract verbs: 'audit,' 'track,' 'optimize,' 'monitor.' The AEO playbook section lists steps without naming a single tool, threshold, cadence, or example brand. The key-takeaways and summary repeat the same abstractions. Concrete nouns—tool names, query examples, specific schema snippet, a named brand outcome—are almost entirely absent outside the one case-study stat.
